risk_title: Political and Governance Risk Remains High
risk_summary: Political and governance risk is rated as High across all assessment periods (approval, last approved rating, and current), indicating persistent institutional fragility affecting refugee administration capacity.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance [Risk Category]: High [Rating at Approval], High [Last Approved Rating ISR Seq. 09], High [Current Rating]

risk_title: Inadequate Organizational Capacity of CCAR in Changing Refugee Protection Context
risk_summary: The Central Alien Registration Authority (CCAR) lacks adequate organizational capacity to undertake its mandate in a changing refugee protection context, with no progress reported as of September 2023, directly affecting visa issuance and refugee management functions.
evidence_quote: CCAR does not have adequate organizational capacity to undertake its mandate in a changing refugee protection context. [Baseline: Oct/2019, Actual (Previous): 30-Sep-2023, Actual (Current): 30-Sep-2023]

risk_title: Absence of Automated Refugee Data Management System
risk_summary: No automated system for monitoring and evaluating refugee data exists as of September 2023, limiting institutional capacity to manage refugee information across participating entities and respond to changing refugee dynamics.
evidence_quote: No automated system for monitoring and evaluating data on refugees. [Baseline: Nov/2019, Actual (Previous): 30-Sep-2023, Actual (Current): 30-Sep-2023]

risk_title: Misalignment of Staff Capacity with Refugee Management Functions
risk_summary: CCAR and provincial CAR staff capacity remains unaligned to functions, jobs, and job descriptions as of September 2023, undermining institutional capacity to manage refugee administration effectively.
evidence_quote: CCAR and CARs staff capacity is not aligned to functions, jobs and job descriptions. [Baseline: Nov/2019, Actual (Previous): 30-Sep-2023, Actual (Current): 30-Sep-2023]
